context: Tests whether the agent performs a Rails architecture review following the rails-architecture-review skill: covering review areas in the correct order, classifying findings by severity, and producing output with all four required fields per finding.

checklist.name: Entry points identified first
checklist.description: The review begins by identifying the entry points of the application (routes, controllers, or public API surface) before examining domain logic or models

checklist.name: Domain logic layer checked
checklist.description: The review explicitly examines where domain/business logic lives — whether it's in models, services, callbacks, or controllers

checklist.name: Models and callbacks inspected
checklist.description: The review addresses model associations, callbacks, or ActiveRecord concerns

checklist.name: High: business logic in callbacks
checklist.description: Business logic embedded in ActiveRecord callbacks is classified as High severity (not Medium or Low)

checklist.name: High: controller multi-step workflow
checklist.description: A controller action that performs multiple sequential domain operations is classified as High severity

checklist.name: Boundary problems prioritised
checklist.description: The findings section leads with boundary violations (cross-layer coupling, misplaced logic) rather than style or naming issues

checklist.name: Medium finding identified
checklist.description: At least one finding is correctly classified as Medium severity (e.g. duplicated workflow, heavy scope/class method, concern mixing responsibilities)

checklist.name: Affected files per finding
checklist.description: Each finding names the specific file(s) where the problem exists

checklist.name: Risk described per finding
checklist.description: Each finding includes a description of the risk — what goes wrong if the issue is not addressed

checklist.name: Improvement per finding
checklist.description: Each finding includes a smallest credible improvement — a specific, actionable refactor suggestion rather than a generic principle

checklist.name: Concerns and helpers checked
checklist.description: The review addresses at least one concern, helper, or module mixin — assessing whether it mixes responsibilities
